steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims that costume designers for modern productions of A Midsummer Night’s Dream sometimes include contemporary elements unintentionally. The correct quotation must show a designer who did not mean to mix in a modern style — it just happened.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘I tend to pay careful attention to a character’s accessories’: describes deliberate attention to period-appropriate details. No unintentional mixing. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Sometimes this means adhering to the historical period’s style, but frequently some flexibility is required’: describes a conscious, intentional choice to deviate — the opposite of unintentional. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Costumes must always reflect the correct historical period’: the designer insists on strict period accuracy and criticizes mixing. No contemporary intrusion at all. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Even though I focused on using the correct materials and techniques… I can clearly see the influence of 1980s fashion in my designs’: the designer aimed for historical accuracy in a production of A Midsummer Night’s Dream but only later realized 1980s style had crept in. That is the definition of an unintentional contemporary element. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
